summary: iOS | Intermittent | DFU/Language Change fails at >90%
scenario: Device intermittently reverts to previous firmware or language after a DFU or language change completes at >90% and a restart.
description_clean: Intermittent failure of DFU or Language Change at >90% completion. After DFU, device shows as up to date but reverts to outdated firmware after restart. After Language Change, device may briefly show new language then restart and revert to previous language. Issue is random and intermittent.
steps: Perform a DFU (Device Firmware Update) or Language Change on the device.
Observe the update progress; note that the failure occurs intermittently at >90% completion.
After DFU completes, verify the device shows as 'up to date' in the settings.
Check the firmware version after restart to see if it has reverted to the outdated firmware.
Alternatively, after a Language Change, observe if the device briefly displays the new language.
Restart the device or allow it to restart automatically.
Check the language setting after restart to see if it has reverted to the previous language.
expected: DFU completes successfully and device remains on the updated firmware after restart. Language Change persists after restart.
actual: Intermittently at >90% completion, the update fails. After DFU, the device shows as up to date but reverts to outdated firmware after restart. After Language Change, the device may briefly show the new language then restart and revert to the previous language.
